title: Cache and Memory Hierarchy Simulator
description: Build a configurable multi-level cache simulator with TLB support. Process memory traces and analyze cache behavior across different configurations. By the end of this project, you will have a working simulator that models direct-mapped, set-associative, and fully-associative caches with LRU and random replacement, multi-level hierarchies with inclusion enforcement, and a complete virtual memory translation pipeline with two-level TLB.
prerequisites: Week 8 Lecture 1: Cache Design and the Memory Hierarchy
Week 8 Lecture 2: Virtual Memory, TLBs, and the Roofline Model
learningObjectives: Implement address decomposition into tag, index, and offset fields for arbitrary cache geometries
Build a fully configurable cache simulator supporting direct-mapped through fully-associative organizations
Implement and compare LRU and random replacement policies
Classify cache misses into compulsory, capacity, and conflict categories using the three-C model
Construct a multi-level cache hierarchy with inclusive cache policy and back-invalidation
Compute Average Memory Access Time (AMAT) for multi-level hierarchies
Simulate virtual-to-physical address translation with a two-level TLB and page table walk

milestones.title: Configurable Cache Simulator
milestones.description: Implement a single-level cache simulator that supports direct-mapped, N-way set-associative (2, 4, 8, 16-way), and fully-associative organizations. The simulator processes address traces and reports detailed hit/miss statistics including compulsory, capacity, and conflict miss classification using the three-C model.
concepts: Address decomposition: tag, index, offset bit fields derived from cache geometry
Direct-mapped cache: one-way set-associative, index selects unique line
Set-associative cache: N lines per set, must search all ways on lookup
Fully-associative cache: single set containing all lines, maximum flexibility
LRU replacement: track access order per set, evict least-recently-used line
Random replacement: select victim uniformly at random from valid lines in set
Three-C miss classification: compulsory (first access to block), capacity (would miss even in fully-associative cache of same size), conflict (miss caused by limited associativity)

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Tuple

import math
import random


class ReplacementPolicy(Enum):
    LRU = "lru"
    RANDOM = "random"


class AccessType(Enum):
    READ = "read"
    WRITE = "write"


class MissType(Enum):
    COMPULSORY = "compulsory"
    CAPACITY = "capacity"
    CONFLICT = "conflict"


@dataclass
class CacheConfig:
    """Configuration for a single cache level.
    
    total_size: Total cache size in bytes (e.g., 32768 for 32KB)
    block_size: Size of each cache block/line in bytes (e.g., 64)
    associativity: Number of ways (1 = direct-mapped, num_sets = fully-associative)
    replacement_policy: LRU or RANDOM
    
    Derived fields (computed in __post_init__):
    - num_blocks: total_size // block_size
    - num_sets: num_blocks // associativity
    - offset_bits: log2(block_size)
    - index_bits: log2(num_sets)
    - tag_bits: address_bits - offset_bits - index_bits
    """
    total_size: int
    block_size: int
    associativity: int
    replacement_policy: ReplacementPolicy = ReplacementPolicy.LRU
    address_bits: int = 32

    # Derived fields
    num_blocks: int = field(init=False)
    num_sets: int = field(init=False)
    offset_bits: int = field(init=False)
    index_bits: int = field(init=False)
    tag_bits: int = field(init=False)

    def __post_init__(self):
        assert self.total_size > 0 and (self.total_size & (self.total_size - 1)) == 0, \
            "total_size must be a positive power of 2"
        assert self.block_size > 0 and (self.block_size & (self.block_size - 1)) == 0, \
            "block_size must be a positive power of 2"
        assert self.total_size >= self.block_size, \
            "total_size must be >= block_size"

        self.num_blocks = self.total_size // self.block_size
        assert self.associativity > 0 and self.associativity <= self.num_blocks, \
            f"associativity must be between 1 and {self.num_blocks}"
        assert self.num_blocks % self.associativity == 0, \
            "num_blocks must be divisible by associativity"

        self.num_sets = self.num_blocks // self.associativity
        self.offset_bits = int(math.log2(self.block_size))
        self.index_bits = int(math.log2(self.num_sets))
        self.tag_bits = self.address_bits - self.offset_bits - self.index_bits

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return (f"Cache: {self.total_size}B, {self.block_size}B blocks, "
                f"{self.associativity}-way, {self.num_sets} sets, "
                f"[tag:{self.tag_bits} | index:{self.index_bits} | offset:{self.offset_bits}]")

@dataclass
class CacheStats:
    """Accumulated statistics for cache simulation."""
    hits: int = 0
    misses: int = 0
    reads: int = 0
    writes: int = 0
    compulsory_misses: int = 0
    capacity_misses: int = 0
    conflict_misses: int = 0
    evictions: int = 0
    dirty_evictions: int = 0

    @property
    def total_accesses(self) -> int:
        return self.hits + self.misses

    @property
    def hit_rate(self) -> float:
        if self.total_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.hits / self.total_accesses

    @property
    def miss_rate(self) -> float:
        return 1.0 - self.hit_rate

    def __str__(self) -> str:

            f"Accesses: {self.total_accesses} | "
            f"Hits: {self.hits} ({self.hit_rate:.2%}) | "

    def process_trace(self, trace: List[Dict]) -> CacheStats:
        """Process a list of memory accesses.

milestones.testCode:
# ============================================================
# Milestone 1 Tests: Configurable Cache Simulator
# ============================================================

# --- Test 1: Address decoding (32KB cache, 64B blocks, 4-way) ---
config = CacheConfig(total_size=32768, block_size=64, associativity=4)
assert config.num_blocks == 512
assert config.num_sets == 128
assert config.offset_bits == 6
assert config.index_bits == 7
assert config.tag_bits == 19

decoder = AddressDecoder(config)
tag, index, offset = decoder.decode(0x0001A3C0)
# 0x0001A3C0 = 0b 0000 0000 0000 0001 1010 0011 1100 0000
# offset [5:0]  = 0b000000 = 0
# index  [12:6] = 0b0001111 = 15
# tag    [31:13]= 0b0000000000000001101 = 13
assert offset == 0, f"Expected offset=0, got {offset}"
assert index == 15, f"Expected index=15, got {index}"
assert tag == 13, f"Expected tag=13, got {tag}"
print('Test 1 passed: Address decoding correct')

# --- Test 2: Direct-mapped cache hit/miss sequence ---
dm_config = CacheConfig(total_size=256, block_size=16, associativity=1)
assert dm_config.num_sets == 16
cache = Cache(dm_config)
# Access block at address 0x00 -> miss (compulsory)
assert cache.access(0x00) == False
# Access same block -> hit
assert cache.access(0x04) == True  # Same block (offset differs)
# Access different block, same set (set 0, different tag) -> miss (conflict)
assert cache.access(0x100) == False  # Maps to same set 0, tag differs
print('Test 2 passed: Direct-mapped hit/miss sequence correct')

# --- Test 3: Set-associative lookup across ways ---
sa_config = CacheConfig(total_size=256, block_size=16, associativity=4)
cache = Cache(sa_config)
# 256B / 16B = 16 blocks, 4-way => 4 sets
assert sa_config.num_sets == 4
# Fill all 4 ways of set 0
for i in range(4):
    cache.access(i * 64)  # Each maps to set 0, different tag
assert cache.stats.misses == 4
assert cache.stats.compulsory_misses == 4
# Re-access first block -> should hit (4-way holds all 4)
assert cache.access(0x00) == True
print('Test 3 passed: 4-way set-associative lookup correct')

# --- Test 4: LRU replacement policy ---
lru_config = CacheConfig(total_size=128, block_size=16, associativity=2)
cache = Cache(lru_config)
# 128B / 16B = 8 blocks, 2-way => 4 sets
# Fill set 0 with 2 blocks
cache.access(0x00)   # Set 0, tag 0 (miss, compulsory)
cache.access(0x40)   # Set 0, tag 1 (miss, compulsory)
# Both ways full. Access new block mapping to set 0:
cache.access(0x80)   # Set 0, tag 2 -> evicts LRU (tag 0)
assert cache.stats.evictions == 1
# tag 0 should be evicted, tag 1 and tag 2 present
assert cache.access(0x00) == False  # tag 0 was evicted -> miss
assert cache.access(0x40) == True   # tag 1 still present -> hit
print('Test 4 passed: LRU replacement correct')

# --- Test 5: Miss classification (three-C model) ---
# Use a tiny direct-mapped cache to force conflict misses
tiny_config = CacheConfig(total_size=64, block_size=16, associativity=1)
cache = Cache(tiny_config)
# 64B / 16B = 4 blocks, direct-mapped => 4 sets
cache.access(0x00)  # Compulsory miss
cache.access(0x40)  # Compulsory miss (different set)
cache.access(0x00)  # Hit
cache.access(0x100) # Maps to set 0 -> evicts 0x00 -> conflict miss
# (fully-assoc of 4 blocks can hold both 0x00 and 0x100)
cache.access(0x00)  # Re-access 0x00 -> conflict miss again
assert cache.stats.compulsory_misses == 2
assert cache.stats.conflict_misses >= 1, f"Expected >= 1 conflict miss, got {cache.stats.conflict_misses}"
print('Test 5 passed: Three-C miss classification correct')

# --- Test 6: Sequential access pattern (high hit rate) ---
seq_config = CacheConfig(total_size=1024, block_size=64, associativity=4)
cache = Cache(seq_config)
for addr in range(0, 1024, 1):  # 1024 sequential byte accesses
    cache.access(addr)
# 1024 bytes / 64B blocks = 16 unique blocks. First access to each block = miss.
# Remaining 1024 - 16 = 1008 accesses hit within cached blocks.
assert cache.stats.compulsory_misses == 16
assert cache.stats.hits == 1024 - 16
assert cache.stats.hit_rate > 0.98
print('Test 6 passed: Sequential pattern gives >98% hit rate')

# --- Test 7: Strided access (stride = block size, all misses initially) ---
stride_config = CacheConfig(total_size=1024, block_size=64, associativity=4)
cache = Cache(stride_config)
# Stride of 64 = one new block per access
for i in range(16):
    cache.access(i * 64)
assert cache.stats.misses == 16
assert cache.stats.compulsory_misses == 16
# Re-traverse: should all hit
for i in range(16):
    cache.access(i * 64)
assert cache.stats.hits == 16
print('Test 7 passed: Stride pattern behavior correct')

# --- Test 8: Write access sets dirty bit ---
w_config = CacheConfig(total_size=256, block_size=16, associativity=2)
cache = Cache(w_config)
cache.access(0x00, AccessType.READ)   # Miss, install clean
cache.access(0x00, AccessType.WRITE)  # Hit, mark dirty
assert cache.stats.reads == 1
assert cache.stats.writes == 1
assert cache.stats.hits == 1
# Force eviction of dirty line
cache.access(0x80, AccessType.READ)   # Same set
cache.access(0x100, AccessType.READ)  # Same set, evicts LRU (0x00, dirty)
assert cache.stats.dirty_evictions == 1
print('Test 8 passed: Dirty bit tracking and eviction correct')

# --- Test 9: Fully-associative cache ---
fa_config = CacheConfig(total_size=256, block_size=16, associativity=16)
assert fa_config.num_sets == 1, "Fully-associative should have 1 set"
cache = Cache(fa_config)
# Fill all 16 ways with unique blocks
for i in range(16):
    cache.access(i * 16)
assert cache.stats.misses == 16
assert cache.stats.compulsory_misses == 16
# All should hit
for i in range(16):
    result = cache.access(i * 16)
    assert result == True, f"Block {i} should hit in fully-associative cache"
print('Test 9 passed: Fully-associative cache correct')

# --- Test 10: Random replacement does not crash, produces valid stats ---
random.seed(42)
rand_config = CacheConfig(
    total_size=256, block_size=16, associativity=4,
    replacement_policy=ReplacementPolicy.RANDOM
)
cache = Cache(rand_config)
for i in range(200):
    cache.access(i * 8)
assert cache.stats.total_accesses == 200
assert cache.stats.hits + cache.stats.misses == 200
assert cache.stats.miss_rate > 0, "Should have some misses"
assert cache.stats.hit_rate >= 0, "Hit rate should be non-negative"
print('Test 10 passed: Random replacement produces valid statistics')

print('\n=== All Milestone 1 tests passed! ===')
hints: For address decoding, remember the bit layout: [tag | index | offset]. Extract offset by masking the lowest offset_bits bits, extract index by shifting right by offset_bits then masking, and extract tag by shifting right by (offset_bits + index_bits).

For LRU, use a monotonically increasing counter per set. Each time a line is accessed (hit or newly installed), set its lru_counter to the incremented counter. The victim is the line with the smallest counter value.

For three-C classification, maintain a shadow fully-associative cache of the same total size. If a miss occurs in the real cache but the block is in the shadow FA cache, it is a conflict miss. If the block is not in the shadow FA cache either, it is a capacity miss. If the block has never been seen before, it is compulsory.

milestones.title: Multi-Level Cache (L1 + L2)
milestones.description: Extend the simulator to model a two-level cache hierarchy (L1 + L2) with configurable sizes and associativity. Implement the inclusive cache policy where L2 is a superset of L1: every block in L1 must also be in L2. On an L1 miss, check L2 before going to memory. On an L2 eviction, back-invalidate the corresponding block in L1 to maintain inclusion. Compute AMAT using configurable hit times and memory access time.
concepts: Multi-level cache hierarchy: L1 (fast, small) and L2 (slower, larger)
Inclusive cache policy: L2 contains a superset of L1 contents at all times
Back-invalidation: when L2 evicts a block, invalidate it in L1 to preserve inclusion
Write-back policy: dirty blocks written back on eviction
Global miss rate vs local miss rate: L2 local miss rate = L2_misses / L2_accesses

from dataclasses import dataclass, field

# Assumes Milestone 1 classes (CacheConfig, Cache, etc.) are available.

@dataclass
class HierarchyConfig:
    """Configuration for a multi-level cache hierarchy."""
    l1_config: CacheConfig
    l2_config: CacheConfig
    l1_hit_time_ns: float = 1.0      # L1 hit latency in nanoseconds
    l2_hit_time_ns: float = 10.0     # L2 hit latency in nanoseconds
    memory_time_ns: float = 100.0    # Main memory access time in nanoseconds

    def __post_init__(self):
        assert self.l2_config.total_size >= self.l1_config.total_size, \
            "L2 must be at least as large as L1"
        assert self.l2_config.block_size == self.l1_config.block_size, \
            "L1 and L2 must have the same block size for inclusion"


@dataclass
class HierarchyStats:
    """Statistics for the full memory hierarchy."""
    l1_hits: int = 0
    l1_misses: int = 0
    l2_hits: int = 0
    l2_misses: int = 0
    memory_accesses: int = 0
    back_invalidations: int = 0
    total_accesses: int = 0

    @property
    def l1_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        if self.total_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l1_hits / self.total_accesses

    @property
    def l1_miss_rate(self) -> float:
        return 1.0 - self.l1_hit_rate

    @property
    def l2_local_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        """L2 hit rate among accesses that reach L2."""
        l2_accesses = self.l2_hits + self.l2_misses
        if l2_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l2_hits / l2_accesses

    @property
    def l2_global_miss_rate(self) -> float:
        """Fraction of all accesses that miss both L1 and L2."""
        if self.total_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l2_misses / self.total_accesses

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"Total accesses: {self.total_accesses}\n"
            f"L1: {self.l1_hits} hits, {self.l1_misses} misses "
            f"({self.l1_hit_rate:.2%} hit rate)\n"
            f"L2: {self.l2_hits} hits, {self.l2_misses} misses "
            f"({self.l2_local_hit_rate:.2%} local hit rate)\n"
            f"Memory accesses: {self.memory_accesses}\n"
            f"Back-invalidations: {self.back_invalidations}"
        )

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Tuple
from enum import Enum
import math
import random


@dataclass
class HierarchyConfig:
    l1_config: CacheConfig
    l2_config: CacheConfig
    l1_hit_time_ns: float = 1.0
    l2_hit_time_ns: float = 10.0
    memory_time_ns: float = 100.0

    def __post_init__(self):
        assert self.l2_config.total_size >= self.l1_config.total_size
        assert self.l2_config.block_size == self.l1_config.block_size


@dataclass
class HierarchyStats:
    l1_hits: int = 0
    l1_misses: int = 0
    l2_hits: int = 0
    l2_misses: int = 0
    memory_accesses: int = 0
    back_invalidations: int = 0
    total_accesses: int = 0

    @property
    def l1_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        if self.total_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l1_hits / self.total_accesses

    @property
    def l1_miss_rate(self) -> float:
        return 1.0 - self.l1_hit_rate

    @property
    def l2_local_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        l2_accesses = self.l2_hits + self.l2_misses
        if l2_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l2_hits / l2_accesses

    @property
    def l2_global_miss_rate(self) -> float:
        if self.total_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l2_misses / self.total_accesses


class MultiLevelCache:
    def __init__(self, config: HierarchyConfig):
        self.config = config
        self.l1 = Cache(config.l1_config)
        self.l2 = Cache(config.l2_config)
        self.stats = HierarchyStats()
        self.l1_block_map: Dict[int, Tuple[int, int]] = {}

    def access(self, address: int, access_type: AccessType = AccessType.READ) -> str:
        self.stats.total_accesses += 1
        block_addr = self.l1.decoder.block_address(address)

        # Check L1
        l1_tag, l1_index, _ = self.l1.decoder.decode(address)
        l1_set = self.l1.sets[l1_index]
        l1_line = l1_set.find_line(l1_tag)

        if l1_line is not None:
            # L1 HIT
            self.stats.l1_hits += 1
            line_idx = l1_set.lines.index(l1_line)
            l1_set.update_lru(line_idx)
            if access_type == AccessType.WRITE:
                l1_line.dirty = True
            return "L1_HIT"

        # L1 MISS - check L2
        self.stats.l1_misses += 1
        l2_tag, l2_index, _ = self.l2.decoder.decode(address)
        l2_set = self.l2.sets[l2_index]
        l2_line = l2_set.find_line(l2_tag)

        if l2_line is not None:
            # L2 HIT
            self.stats.l2_hits += 1
            l2_line_idx = l2_set.lines.index(l2_line)
            l2_set.update_lru(l2_line_idx)
            self._install_in_l1(address, access_type)
            return "L2_HIT"

        # L2 MISS - go to memory
        self.stats.l2_misses += 1
        self.stats.memory_accesses += 1
        evicted_block = self._install_in_l2(address, access_type)
        if evicted_block is not None:
            self._back_invalidate(evicted_block)
        self._install_in_l1(address, access_type)
        return "MEMORY"

    def _install_in_l1(self, address: int, access_type: AccessType):
        l1_tag, l1_index, _ = self.l1.decoder.decode(address)
        block_addr = self.l1.decoder.block_address(address)
        l1_set = self.l1.sets[l1_index]

        victim_idx = l1_set.find_victim(self.config.l1_config.replacement_policy)
        victim = l1_set.lines[victim_idx]

        if victim.valid:
            # Remove old block from tracking map
            old_block_addr = (victim.tag << self.config.l1_config.index_bits) | l1_index
            self.l1_block_map.pop(old_block_addr, None)

        victim.valid = True
        victim.tag = l1_tag
        victim.dirty = (access_type == AccessType.WRITE)
        l1_set.update_lru(victim_idx)

        self.l1_block_map[block_addr] = (l1_index, victim_idx)

    def _install_in_l2(self, address: int, access_type: AccessType) -> Optional[int]:
        l2_tag, l2_index, _ = self.l2.decoder.decode(address)
        l2_set = self.l2.sets[l2_index]

        victim_idx = l2_set.find_victim(self.config.l2_config.replacement_policy)
        victim = l2_set.lines[victim_idx]

        evicted_block = None
        if victim.valid:
            evicted_block = (victim.tag << (self.config.l2_config.index_bits + self.config.l2_config.offset_bits)) >> self.config.l2_config.offset_bits
            evicted_block = (victim.tag << self.config.l2_config.index_bits) | l2_index

        victim.valid = True
        victim.tag = l2_tag
        victim.dirty = (access_type == AccessType.WRITE)
        l2_set.update_lru(victim_idx)

        return evicted_block

    def _back_invalidate(self, block_addr: int):
        if block_addr in self.l1_block_map:
            l1_index, way_index = self.l1_block_map[block_addr]
            line = self.l1.sets[l1_index].lines[way_index]
            line.valid = False
            line.dirty = False
            del self.l1_block_map[block_addr]
            self.stats.back_invalidations += 1

    def compute_amat(self) -> float:
        l1_miss_rate = self.stats.l1_miss_rate
        l2_accesses = self.stats.l2_hits + self.stats.l2_misses
        l2_local_miss_rate = 0.0
        if l2_accesses > 0:
            l2_local_miss_rate = self.stats.l2_misses / l2_accesses

        amat = (self.config.l1_hit_time_ns
                + l1_miss_rate * (self.config.l2_hit_time_ns
                                  + l2_local_miss_rate * self.config.memory_time_ns))
        return amat

    def process_trace(self, trace: List[Dict]) -> Tuple[HierarchyStats, float]:
        for entry in trace:
            addr = int(entry["address"], 16)
            atype = AccessType.READ if entry["type"] == "read" else AccessType.WRITE
            self.access(addr, atype)
        return self.stats, self.compute_amat()

    def verify_inclusion(self) -> bool:
        for block_addr in self.l1_block_map:
            addr = block_addr << self.config.l1_config.offset_bits
            l2_tag, l2_index, _ = self.l2.decoder.decode(addr)
            l2_set = self.l2.sets[l2_index]
            found = any(line.valid and line.tag == l2_tag for line in l2_set.lines)
            if not found:
                return False
        return True

# ============================================================
# Milestone 2 Tests: Multi-Level Cache (L1 + L2)
# ============================================================

# --- Setup ---
l1_cfg = CacheConfig(total_size=256, block_size=16, associativity=2)
l2_cfg = CacheConfig(total_size=1024, block_size=16, associativity=4)
hier_cfg = HierarchyConfig(
    l1_config=l1_cfg, l2_config=l2_cfg,
    l1_hit_time_ns=1.0, l2_hit_time_ns=10.0, memory_time_ns=100.0
)

# --- Test 1: Cold start goes to memory ---
ml = MultiLevelCache(hier_cfg)
result = ml.access(0x00)
assert result == "MEMORY", f"First access should go to memory, got {result}"
assert ml.stats.l1_misses == 1
assert ml.stats.l2_misses == 1
assert ml.stats.memory_accesses == 1
print('Test 1 passed: Cold start accesses memory')

# --- Test 2: Second access hits L1 ---
result = ml.access(0x04)  # Same block as 0x00
assert result == "L1_HIT", f"Same block should hit L1, got {result}"
assert ml.stats.l1_hits == 1
print('Test 2 passed: Repeated access hits L1')

# --- Test 3: L1 miss caught by L2 ---
ml2 = MultiLevelCache(hier_cfg)
# Access enough blocks to fill L1 set, then evict from L1 but not L2
# L1: 256B/16B = 16 blocks, 2-way => 8 sets
# L2: 1024B/16B = 64 blocks, 4-way => 16 sets
ml2.access(0x00)    # Miss L1, miss L2 -> MEMORY (set 0 in L1)
ml2.access(0x80)    # Miss L1, miss L2 -> MEMORY (set 0 in L1, different tag)
ml2.access(0x100)   # Miss L1 (evicts 0x00 from L1), miss L2 -> MEMORY
# Now 0x00 is evicted from L1 but should still be in L2
result = ml2.access(0x00)  # Should miss L1, hit L2
assert result == "L2_HIT", f"Expected L2_HIT after L1 eviction, got {result}"
assert ml2.stats.l2_hits >= 1, "L2 should catch L1 misses"
print('Test 3 passed: L2 catches L1 misses')

# --- Test 4: Inclusion invariant holds ---
ml3 = MultiLevelCache(hier_cfg)
for i in range(50):
    ml3.access(i * 16)
assert ml3.verify_inclusion(), "Inclusion invariant violated!"
print('Test 4 passed: Inclusion invariant maintained')

# --- Test 5: Back-invalidation on L2 eviction ---
ml4 = MultiLevelCache(hier_cfg)
# Fill up L2 with many blocks, then force an L2 eviction
# L2: 64 blocks, 4-way, 16 sets. Fill set 0 with 4 blocks, then add 5th.
addresses_set0 = []
for i in range(5):
    # Addresses that map to L2 set 0
    # With 16 sets, index_bits=4, offset_bits=4
    # Set 0 blocks: tags 0,1,2,3,4 => addresses 0x000, 0x100, 0x200, 0x300, 0x400
    addr = i * 256  # 256 = 16 sets * 16 block_size
    ml4.access(addr)
# 5th access to set 0 should evict something from L2
# After enough evictions, check inclusion
assert ml4.verify_inclusion(), "Inclusion violated after potential L2 eviction"
print('Test 5 passed: Back-invalidation preserves inclusion')

# --- Test 6: AMAT calculation ---
ml5 = MultiLevelCache(hier_cfg)
# Engineer known hit rates
# 10 accesses: 7 L1 hits, 2 L2 hits, 1 memory
# First access each unique block = miss. We need careful control.
# Simple: access same address 10 times. First = memory, rest = L1 hit.
for _ in range(10):
    ml5.access(0x00)
assert ml5.stats.l1_hits == 9
assert ml5.stats.l1_misses == 1
assert ml5.stats.l2_misses == 1
amat = ml5.compute_amat()
# L1 miss rate = 1/10 = 0.1
# L2 local miss rate = 1/1 = 1.0 (only 1 access to L2, and it missed)
# AMAT = 1.0 + 0.1 * (10.0 + 1.0 * 100.0) = 1.0 + 0.1 * 110.0 = 12.0
assert abs(amat - 12.0) < 0.01, f"Expected AMAT=12.0, got {amat}"
print('Test 6 passed: AMAT calculation correct')

# --- Test 7: AMAT with mixed L1/L2 hits ---
ml6_l1 = CacheConfig(total_size=128, block_size=16, associativity=2)
ml6_l2 = CacheConfig(total_size=512, block_size=16, associativity=4)
ml6_cfg = HierarchyConfig(
    l1_config=ml6_l1, l2_config=ml6_l2,
    l1_hit_time_ns=2.0, l2_hit_time_ns=15.0, memory_time_ns=200.0
)
ml6 = MultiLevelCache(ml6_cfg)
# Sequential accesses to build up meaningful stats
for i in range(256):
    ml6.access(i)  # Sequential bytes
amat = ml6.compute_amat()
assert amat >= ml6_cfg.l1_hit_time_ns, "AMAT cannot be less than L1 hit time"
assert amat < ml6_cfg.memory_time_ns, "AMAT should be much less than memory time"
print(f'Test 7 passed: AMAT = {amat:.2f} ns (between L1 hit and memory time)')

# --- Test 8: Per-level statistics consistency ---
ml7 = MultiLevelCache(hier_cfg)
for i in range(100):
    ml7.access(i * 4)
assert ml7.stats.total_accesses == 100
assert ml7.stats.l1_hits + ml7.stats.l1_misses == 100
assert ml7.stats.l2_hits + ml7.stats.l2_misses == ml7.stats.l1_misses
assert ml7.stats.memory_accesses == ml7.stats.l2_misses
print('Test 8 passed: Per-level statistics are consistent')

print('\n=== All Milestone 2 tests passed! ===')
hints: The key challenge is maintaining the inclusion invariant. When L2 evicts a block, check if that block exists in L1 (use the l1_block_map dictionary for O(1) lookup). If found, invalidate the L1 line and remove it from the map.

For _install_in_l2, you need to reconstruct the evicted block's address from its tag and the set index. The block address is (tag << index_bits) | set_index. Return this so the caller can check for back-invalidation.
AMAT uses LOCAL miss rates for L2, not global. L2 local miss rate = L2_misses / (L2_hits + L2_misses), which is the miss rate among accesses that actually reach L2.

milestones.title: TLB Simulation
milestones.description: Implement a virtual memory translation system with a two-level TLB (L1 DTLB + L2 TLB) and page table walk simulation. The simulator translates virtual addresses to physical addresses, models TLB lookup latency, and tracks page table walk statistics. Support configurable page sizes (4KB standard pages and 2MB huge pages) and TLB sizes.
concepts: Virtual-to-physical address translation: virtual page number (VPN) maps to physical frame number (PFN)
TLB: hardware cache of recent page table entries, indexed by VPN
Two-level TLB: L1 DTLB (small, fast, ~1 cycle) + L2 TLB (larger, slower, ~10 cycles)
Page table walk: on TLB miss, traverse the page table in memory to find the mapping
Page size: 4KB (12-bit offset) standard, 2MB (21-bit offset) huge pages reduce TLB pressure
Effective access time: TLB_hit_time + TLB_miss_rate * page_table_walk_time + memory_access_time

@dataclass

    """Configuration for a single TLB level."""
    num_entries: int       # Number of TLB entries (e.g., 64)
    associativity: int     # Associativity (e.g., 4-way, or num_entries for fully-assoc)
    hit_time_cycles: int   # Hit latency in cycles

    num_sets: int = field(init=False)

        assert self.num_entries > 0
        assert self.associativity > 0 and self.associativity <= self.num_entries
        assert self.num_entries % self.associativity == 0
        self.num_sets = self.num_entries // self.associativity

    address_bits: int = 32             # Virtual address space (32-bit)

class VirtualMemorySystem:
    """Complete virtual memory translation system with two-level TLB.
    
    Translation pipeline:
    1. Extract VPN from virtual address.
    2. Check L1 DTLB.
    3. On L1 DTLB miss, check L2 TLB.
    4. On L2 TLB miss, perform page table walk.
    5. Install mapping in both TLBs.
    6. Return physical address.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: VirtualMemoryConfig):
        self.config = config
        self.l1_dtlb = TLB(config.l1_dtlb, config.offset_bits, config.vpn_bits)
        self.l2_tlb = TLB(config.l2_tlb, config.offset_bits, config.vpn_bits)
        self.page_table = PageTable(config)
        self.stats = TranslationStats()

    def translate(self, virtual_address: int) -> Tuple[int, int]:

        3. If L1 DTLB miss, check L2 TLB.
           - If hit: install in L1 DTLB, record L2 TLB hit, return.
        4. If L2 TLB miss, perform page table walk.
           - Install in both L1 DTLB and L2 TLB.
           - Record page table walk.
        5. Return (physical_address, latency_in_cycles).
        
        Physical address = (PFN << offset_bits) | offset
        
        Latency:
        - L1 DTLB hit: l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles
        - L2 TLB hit: l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles + l2_tlb.hit_time_cycles
        - Page table walk: l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles + l2_tlb.hit_time_cycles + walk_cycles
        
        Returns:
            Tuple of (physical_address, latency_in_cycles)
        """

    def _extract_vpn_offset(self, virtual_address: int) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        """Extract VPN and page offset from a virtual address."""
        offset_mask = (1 << self.config.offset_bits) - 1
        offset = virtual_address & offset_mask
        vpn = virtual_address >> self.config.offset_bits
        return vpn, offset

    def process_trace(self, trace: List[Dict]) -> TranslationStats:
        """Process a memory trace through the virtual memory system."""
        for entry in trace:
            addr = int(entry["address"], 16)
            self.translate(addr)
        self.stats.unique_pages_accessed = len(self.page_table.entries)
        return self.stats

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Tuple
from enum import Enum
import math
import random


@dataclass
class TLBConfig:
    num_entries: int
    associativity: int
    hit_time_cycles: int
    num_sets: int = field(init=False)

    def __post_init__(self):
        assert self.num_entries > 0
        assert self.associativity > 0 and self.associativity <= self.num_entries
        assert self.num_entries % self.associativity == 0
        self.num_sets = self.num_entries // self.associativity


@dataclass
class VirtualMemoryConfig:
    page_size: int = 4096
    address_bits: int = 32
    physical_bits: int = 32
    l1_dtlb: TLBConfig = field(default_factory=lambda: TLBConfig(
        num_entries=32, associativity=32, hit_time_cycles=1
    ))
    l2_tlb: TLBConfig = field(default_factory=lambda: TLBConfig(
        num_entries=128, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=10
    ))
    page_table_walk_cycles: int = 100
    memory_access_cycles: int = 1
    offset_bits: int = field(init=False)
    vpn_bits: int = field(init=False)

    def __post_init__(self):
        self.offset_bits = int(math.log2(self.page_size))
        self.vpn_bits = self.address_bits - self.offset_bits


@dataclass
class PageTableEntry:
    vpn: int
    pfn: int
    valid: bool = True
    dirty: bool = False
    accessed: bool = False
    permissions: str = "rwx"


@dataclass
class TLBEntry:
    valid: bool = False
    vpn: int = 0
    pfn: int = 0
    dirty: bool = False
    lru_counter: int = 0


class TLBSet:
    def __init__(self, associativity: int):
        self.entries: List[TLBEntry] = [TLBEntry() for _ in range(associativity)]
        self.access_counter: int = 0

    def lookup(self, vpn: int) -> Optional[TLBEntry]:
        for entry in self.entries:
            if entry.valid and entry.vpn == vpn:
                return entry
        return None

    def install(self, vpn: int, pfn: int) -> Optional[int]:
        # Prefer invalid entries
        for i, entry in enumerate(self.entries):
            if not entry.valid:
                self.access_counter += 1
                entry.valid = True
                entry.vpn = vpn
                entry.pfn = pfn
                entry.lru_counter = self.access_counter
                return None

        # All valid: evict LRU
        min_counter = self.entries[0].lru_counter
        min_index = 0
        for i, entry in enumerate(self.entries):
            if entry.lru_counter < min_counter:
                min_counter = entry.lru_counter
                min_index = i

        evicted_vpn = self.entries[min_index].vpn
        self.access_counter += 1
        self.entries[min_index].valid = True
        self.entries[min_index].vpn = vpn
        self.entries[min_index].pfn = pfn
        self.entries[min_index].lru_counter = self.access_counter
        return evicted_vpn

    def update_lru(self, entry_index: int):
        self.access_counter += 1
        self.entries[entry_index].lru_counter = self.access_counter


class TLB:
    def __init__(self, config: TLBConfig, offset_bits: int, vpn_bits: int):
        self.config = config
        self.offset_bits = offset_bits
        self.vpn_bits = vpn_bits
        self.sets: List[TLBSet] = [
            TLBSet(config.associativity) for _ in range(config.num_sets)
        ]
        self.hits: int = 0
        self.misses: int = 0
        self.index_bits = int(math.log2(config.num_sets)) if config.num_sets > 1 else 0
        self.index_mask = (1 << self.index_bits) - 1

    def _get_set_index(self, vpn: int) -> int:
        if self.config.num_sets == 1:
            return 0
        return vpn & self.index_mask

    def lookup(self, vpn: int) -> Optional[int]:
        set_index = self._get_set_index(vpn)
        tlb_set = self.sets[set_index]
        entry = tlb_set.lookup(vpn)
        if entry is not None:
            self.hits += 1
            entry_idx = tlb_set.entries.index(entry)
            tlb_set.update_lru(entry_idx)
            return entry.pfn
        self.misses += 1
        return None

    def install(self, vpn: int, pfn: int) -> Optional[int]:
        set_index = self._get_set_index(vpn)
        return self.sets[set_index].install(vpn, pfn)

    @property
    def hit_rate(self) -> float:
        total = self.hits + self.misses
        if total == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.hits / total


class PageTable:
    def __init__(self, config: VirtualMemoryConfig):
        self.config = config
        self.entries: Dict[int, PageTableEntry] = {}
        self.next_pfn: int = 0
        self.walks: int = 0

    def translate(self, vpn: int) -> PageTableEntry:
        self.walks += 1
        if vpn in self.entries:
            self.entries[vpn].accessed = True
            return self.entries[vpn]
        pte = PageTableEntry(vpn=vpn, pfn=self.next_pfn)
        self.next_pfn += 1
        self.entries[vpn] = pte
        return pte


@dataclass
class TranslationStats:
    total_translations: int = 0
    l1_dtlb_hits: int = 0
    l2_tlb_hits: int = 0
    page_table_walks: int = 0
    unique_pages_accessed: int = 0

    @property
    def l1_dtlb_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        if self.total_translations == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l1_dtlb_hits / self.total_translations

    @property
    def l2_tlb_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        l2_accesses = self.l2_tlb_hits + self.page_table_walks
        if l2_accesses == 0:
            return 0.0
        return self.l2_tlb_hits / l2_accesses

    @property
    def overall_tlb_hit_rate(self) -> float:
        if self.total_translations == 0:
            return 0.0
        return (self.l1_dtlb_hits + self.l2_tlb_hits) / self.total_translations


class VirtualMemorySystem:
    def __init__(self, config: VirtualMemoryConfig):
        self.config = config
        self.l1_dtlb = TLB(config.l1_dtlb, config.offset_bits, config.vpn_bits)
        self.l2_tlb = TLB(config.l2_tlb, config.offset_bits, config.vpn_bits)
        self.page_table = PageTable(config)
        self.stats = TranslationStats()

    def translate(self, virtual_address: int) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        self.stats.total_translations += 1
        vpn, offset = self._extract_vpn_offset(virtual_address)

        # Check L1 DTLB
        pfn = self.l1_dtlb.lookup(vpn)
        if pfn is not None:
            self.stats.l1_dtlb_hits += 1
            phys_addr = (pfn << self.config.offset_bits) | offset
            latency = self.config.l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles + self.config.memory_access_cycles
            return phys_addr, latency

        # L1 DTLB miss - check L2 TLB
        pfn = self.l2_tlb.lookup(vpn)
        if pfn is not None:
            self.stats.l2_tlb_hits += 1
            self.l1_dtlb.install(vpn, pfn)
            phys_addr = (pfn << self.config.offset_bits) | offset
            latency = (self.config.l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles
                       + self.config.l2_tlb.hit_time_cycles
                       + self.config.memory_access_cycles)
            return phys_addr, latency

        # L2 TLB miss - page table walk
        pte = self.page_table.translate(vpn)
        self.stats.page_table_walks += 1
        pfn = pte.pfn
        self.l1_dtlb.install(vpn, pfn)
        self.l2_tlb.install(vpn, pfn)
        phys_addr = (pfn << self.config.offset_bits) | offset
        latency = (self.config.l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles
                   + self.config.l2_tlb.hit_time_cycles
                   + self.config.page_table_walk_cycles
                   + self.config.memory_access_cycles)
        return phys_addr, latency

    def _extract_vpn_offset(self, virtual_address: int) -> Tuple[int, int]:
        offset_mask = (1 << self.config.offset_bits) - 1
        offset = virtual_address & offset_mask
        vpn = virtual_address >> self.config.offset_bits
        return vpn, offset

    def process_trace(self, trace: List[Dict]) -> TranslationStats:
        for entry in trace:
            addr = int(entry["address"], 16)
            self.translate(addr)
        self.stats.unique_pages_accessed = len(self.page_table.entries)
        return self.stats

    def compute_effective_access_time(self) -> float:
        l1_hr = self.stats.l1_dtlb_hit_rate
        l1_mr = 1.0 - l1_hr
        l2_local_hr = self.stats.l2_tlb_hit_rate
        l2_local_mr = 1.0 - l2_local_hr

        eat = (l1_hr * self.config.l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles
               + l1_mr * l2_local_hr * (self.config.l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles
                                        + self.config.l2_tlb.hit_time_cycles)
               + l1_mr * l2_local_mr * (self.config.l1_dtlb.hit_time_cycles
                                        + self.config.l2_tlb.hit_time_cycles
                                        + self.config.page_table_walk_cycles)
               + self.config.memory_access_cycles)
        return eat

# ============================================================
# Milestone 3 Tests: TLB Simulation
# ============================================================

# --- Test 1: Basic VPN/offset extraction (4KB pages) ---
vm_config = VirtualMemoryConfig(page_size=4096)
assert vm_config.offset_bits == 12
assert vm_config.vpn_bits == 20

vm = VirtualMemorySystem(vm_config)
vpn, offset = vm._extract_vpn_offset(0x12345678)
assert vpn == 0x12345, f"Expected VPN=0x12345, got {hex(vpn)}"
assert offset == 0x678, f"Expected offset=0x678, got {hex(offset)}"
print('Test 1 passed: VPN/offset extraction correct for 4KB pages')

# --- Test 2: Basic VPN/offset extraction (2MB huge pages) ---
huge_config = VirtualMemoryConfig(
    page_size=2 * 1024 * 1024,  # 2MB
    l1_dtlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=16, associativity=16, hit_time_cycles=1),
    l2_tlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=64, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=10)
)
assert huge_config.offset_bits == 21
huge_vm = VirtualMemorySystem(huge_config)
vpn, offset = huge_vm._extract_vpn_offset(0x12345678)
assert vpn == 0x12345678 >> 21
assert offset == 0x12345678 & ((1 << 21) - 1)
print('Test 2 passed: VPN/offset extraction correct for 2MB huge pages')

# --- Test 3: Sequential page accesses (high TLB hit rate) ---
vm = VirtualMemorySystem(VirtualMemoryConfig(page_size=4096))
# Access sequential addresses within the same page -> all hit after first
for i in range(100):
    vm.translate(0x1000 + i * 4)  # All within page VPN=1
assert vm.stats.l1_dtlb_hits == 99, f"Expected 99 L1 DTLB hits, got {vm.stats.l1_dtlb_hits}"
assert vm.stats.page_table_walks == 1
assert vm.stats.l1_dtlb_hit_rate == 0.99
print('Test 3 passed: Sequential same-page accesses give 99% L1 DTLB hit rate')

# --- Test 4: Access across many pages (tests TLB capacity) ---
small_tlb_config = VirtualMemoryConfig(
    page_size=4096,
    l1_dtlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=8, associativity=8, hit_time_cycles=1),
    l2_tlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=32, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=10)
)
vm2 = VirtualMemorySystem(small_tlb_config)
# Access 64 different pages, then re-access them
for i in range(64):
    vm2.translate(i * 4096)  # Each on a different page
# First 8 pages fit in L1 DTLB, first 32 in L2 TLB
# Re-access page 0 (likely evicted from L1 DTLB if >8 pages touched)
assert vm2.stats.page_table_walks > 0, "Should have page table walks for cold pages"
assert vm2.stats.unique_pages_accessed == 0 or True  # Will be set by process_trace
print('Test 4 passed: Multi-page access generates expected walks')

# --- Test 5: L2 TLB catches L1 DTLB misses ---
vm3 = VirtualMemorySystem(VirtualMemoryConfig(
    page_size=4096,
    l1_dtlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=4, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=1),
    l2_tlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=32, associativity=32, hit_time_cycles=10)
))
# Access 8 different pages (exceeds L1 DTLB of 4 entries)
for i in range(8):
    vm3.translate(i * 4096)
# Re-access first page (evicted from L1 DTLB, but should be in L2 TLB)
vm3.translate(0)
assert vm3.stats.l2_tlb_hits >= 1, "L2 TLB should catch L1 DTLB misses"
print('Test 5 passed: L2 TLB catches L1 DTLB misses')

# --- Test 6: Physical address construction ---
vm4 = VirtualMemorySystem(VirtualMemoryConfig(page_size=4096))
phys_addr, latency = vm4.translate(0x00001234)
# VPN = 1, offset = 0x234
# PFN assigned by page table (starts at 0)
expected_offset = 0x234
assert (phys_addr & 0xFFF) == expected_offset, \
    f"Offset should be preserved: expected {hex(expected_offset)}, got {hex(phys_addr & 0xFFF)}"
print('Test 6 passed: Physical address correctly preserves page offset')

# --- Test 7: Random pages cause low TLB hit rate ---
random.seed(123)
vm5 = VirtualMemorySystem(VirtualMemoryConfig(
    page_size=4096,
    l1_dtlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=32, associativity=32, hit_time_cycles=1),
    l2_tlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=128, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=10)
))
# Access 500 random pages in a large range
for _ in range(500):
    random_page = random.randint(0, 10000)
    vm5.translate(random_page * 4096)
# With 10001 possible pages and 128+32 TLB entries, hit rate should be low
assert vm5.stats.overall_tlb_hit_rate < 0.5, \
    f"Random access should have low TLB hit rate, got {vm5.stats.overall_tlb_hit_rate:.2%}"
print('Test 7 passed: Random page access gives low TLB hit rate')

# --- Test 8: Huge pages reduce TLB misses ---
# Compare 4KB vs 2MB pages for sequential access over 4MB range
vm_4k = VirtualMemorySystem(VirtualMemoryConfig(
    page_size=4096,
    l1_dtlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=32, associativity=32, hit_time_cycles=1),
    l2_tlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=128, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=10)
))
vm_2m = VirtualMemorySystem(VirtualMemoryConfig(
    page_size=2 * 1024 * 1024,
    l1_dtlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=32, associativity=32, hit_time_cycles=1),
    l2_tlb=TLBConfig(num_entries=128, associativity=4, hit_time_cycles=10)
))
for addr in range(0, 4 * 1024 * 1024, 4096):  # Step through 4MB, one access per 4K
    vm_4k.translate(addr)
    vm_2m.translate(addr)
# 4KB pages: 1024 unique pages for 4MB range
# 2MB pages: 2 unique pages for 4MB range
assert vm_2m.stats.page_table_walks < vm_4k.stats.page_table_walks, \
    "Huge pages should require fewer page table walks"
assert vm_2m.stats.l1_dtlb_hit_rate > vm_4k.stats.l1_dtlb_hit_rate, \
    "Huge pages should give higher DTLB hit rate"
print(f'Test 8 passed: Huge pages reduce walks ({vm_2m.stats.page_table_walks} vs {vm_4k.stats.page_table_walks})')

print('\n=== All Milestone 3 tests passed! ===')
hints: The translation pipeline has three cases: L1 DTLB hit (fastest, just return), L2 TLB hit (install into L1 DTLB, then return), and full miss (page table walk, install into both L2 TLB and L1 DTLB). Always construct the physical address as (PFN << offset_bits) | offset.
For TLBSet.install(), always check for invalid entries first to avoid unnecessary evictions. If you must evict, find the entry with the smallest lru_counter (same LRU scheme as the cache sets).

Huge pages (2MB) dramatically reduce TLB pressure because each TLB entry covers 512x more address space. A 32-entry TLB with 2MB pages covers 64MB, while 4KB pages only cover 128KB. This is why servers use huge pages for large memory workloads.

memoryTraces.name: sequential
memoryTraces.description: Sequential byte accesses starting from address 0x10000. Each access reads the next byte. This pattern exhibits excellent spatial locality: the first access to each 64-byte cache line is a compulsory miss, but the next 63 accesses to that line all hit. Expected cache hit rate: ~98.4% for a cache with 64-byte blocks. The full trace would contain 1000 entries; 50 representative entries are shown.
memoryTraces.pattern: addr = 0x10000 + i for i in range(1000)

memoryTraces.name: strided
memoryTraces.description: Accesses with a stride of 64 bytes (one cache line), starting from 0x20000. Each access lands on a new cache line, so every access is a compulsory miss on the first pass. This pattern tests associativity: if the stride causes many addresses to map to the same cache set, a direct-mapped cache will thrash while a set-associative cache may absorb the conflict. The full trace would contain 1000 entries; 50 representative entries are shown.
memoryTraces.pattern: addr = 0x20000 + (i * 64) for i in range(500), then repeat

memoryTraces.name: random
memoryTraces.description: Pseudo-random accesses within a 1MB range (0x00100000 to 0x001FFFFF). This pattern has poor spatial and temporal locality, representing worst-case cache behavior. Random accesses stress the replacement policy and expose capacity limitations. Expected hit rate depends heavily on cache size relative to the 1MB working set. The full trace would contain 1000 entries; 50 representative entries are shown (generated with seed 42 for reproducibility).
memoryTraces.pattern: random.seed(42); addr = 0x100000 + random.randint(0, 0xFFFFF) for each access

categories.name: Correctness
categories.description: Code produces correct results for all test cases and handles edge cases properly.

criteria.description: Correctly extracts tag, index, and offset fields for all cache geometries (direct-mapped, set-associative, fully-associative). Handles varying block sizes, set counts, and address widths.
Correctly identifies hits (valid line with matching tag) and misses. Searches all ways in a set for set-associative caches. Handles the single-set case for fully-associative caches.
LRU correctly evicts the least-recently-used line using monotonic counters. Random selects uniformly. Both policies prefer invalid (empty) slots before evicting valid lines.

Correctly classifies misses as compulsory (first access to block), capacity (fully-associative shadow cache also misses), or conflict (shadow cache hits but real cache misses due to limited associativity).
L1 miss correctly checks L2. L2 miss correctly fetches from memory. Block installation proceeds from lower to higher levels. Statistics per level are accurate and consistent.
L2 eviction correctly triggers L1 invalidation of the corresponding block. Inclusion invariant (verify_inclusion) holds at all times. Back-invalidation counter is accurate.

criteria.item: TLB lookup and page table walk
criteria.description: Two-level TLB correctly checks L1 DTLB first, then L2 TLB. TLB miss triggers page table walk. Mappings are installed in both TLB levels after a walk. Physical address construction preserves the page offset.

categories.name: Analysis
categories.description: Quantitative analysis of cache behavior, performance metrics, and design tradeoffs.

criteria.description: Correctly computes AMAT = L1_hit_time + L1_miss_rate * (L2_hit_time + L2_local_miss_rate * memory_time). Uses local miss rates for L2, not global. Result matches expected values for known workloads.
Reports and explains hit rates for sequential (high), strided (medium, depends on associativity), and random (low) access patterns. Identifies why each pattern produces its characteristic behavior.
Demonstrates that increasing associativity reduces conflict misses but not compulsory or capacity misses. Shows diminishing returns beyond a certain associativity for a given workload.
Compares effective access time for 4KB vs 2MB pages. Explains how huge pages reduce TLB misses for large working sets. Computes and explains effective access time.

categories.name: Design
categories.description: Code organization, use of Python best practices, and extensibility.

criteria.description: All configuration and state classes use @dataclass with proper type annotations. Enums used for categorical values (ReplacementPolicy, AccessType, MissType). No raw strings or magic numbers.
Clear separation between address decoding (AddressDecoder), storage (CacheSet/CacheLine), policy (replacement logic), and statistics (CacheStats). Each class has a single responsibility.

MultiLevelCache composes Cache objects rather than duplicating logic. VirtualMemorySystem composes TLB and PageTable. Adding a third cache level or a different replacement policy would require minimal changes.
Handles direct-mapped (1-way), fully-associative (1 set), empty caches, single-entry TLBs, and boundary addresses. Assertions in __post_init__ catch invalid configurations early.

categories.name: Documentation
categories.description: Code comments, docstrings, and inline explanations of architectural decisions.

criteria.description: All public methods have docstrings explaining purpose, parameters, and return values. Complex algorithms (LRU, miss classification, back-invalidation) have step-by-step comments.
Bit manipulation, address reconstruction, and the three-C classification shadow cache are explained with inline comments. Mathematical formulas (AMAT, EAT) are stated before implementation.

Brief written analysis (as code comments or print statements) explaining the observed hit rates, the impact of associativity, and the benefit of huge pages. Connects simulation results to the theory from lectures.
